claim_text: It is unclear why we need unitwise and interunit meta-autoencoders.
llm_notes: The wording differs but the core concern about necessity/justification of using multiple meta-encoders aligns.
llm_rationale: Both reviewer claims question the necessity or rationale for using multiple meta-encoders (unitwise/inter-unit) or choosing between them. They express the same underlying concern: whether having multiple meta-autoencoders is necessary or beneficial, implying a similar issue about the methodological justification for multiple encoders.
matched_claim_text: 2-Why is it necessary to use two different meta encoders? Can we use only one of them, such as either the "unit-wise" meta-autoencoder or the "inter-unit" meta-autoencoder only?
source_sentence: It is unclear why we need unitwise and interunit meta-autoencoders.
title: Metanetwork: A novel approach to interpreting ANNs

claim_text: The method only works on semi-linear PDEs. This is actually a very strong assumption and limitation. The authors should discuss the extension to nonlinear PDEs.
llm_rationale: Both claims address the method's applicability to nonlinear PDEs and limitations/extensions to nonlinear problems. However, the first claim emphasizes that the method only works on semi-linear PDEs and calls for extension to nonlinear PDEs, while the matched claim seeks clarification on whether nonlinear PDEs are handleable and potential extensions. They pertain to the same general concern (limitations regarding nonlinear PDEs) but do not express the exact same specific issue (one asserts a limitation and asks for extension; the other requests clarification and extensions). Therefore related but not the same concern.
matched_claim_text: Unclear whether NEKM can handle nonlinear PDEs; need clarification on limitations and potential extensions to nonlinear problems.
source_sentence: The method only works on semi-linear PDEs. This is actually a very strong assumption and limitation. The authors should discuss the extension to nonlinear PDEs.
title: Neural Evolutionary Kernel Method: A Knowledge-Based Learning Architechture for Evolutionary PDEs

claim_text: Figure 1 has no proper caption, which makes it hard to read and understand the figure.
llm_rationale: Both claims concern the readability/clarity of Figure 1. The first notes the lack of a proper caption making it hard to read; the second questions whether Figure 1 is properly labeled or referenced in Section 4.1, which relates to the same issue of figure clarity and usability. The concerns are not about different aspects, but about how the figure is presented and understood.
source_sentence: - Figure 1 has no proper caption, which makes it hard to read and understand the figure.
title: Metanetwork: A novel approach to interpreting ANNs

claim_text:
The biggest weakness in the paper seems to be that a critical part of it is missing. The experimental setup in section 4.1 states 
> We control the size of the local neighborhood by controlling the diagonal lengths via the $r$ parameter mentioned in section 3.2

But section 3.2 makes no mention of a parameter $r$, and it is difficult to make a full evaluation of the experiments in section 4 absent a definition of $r$.
llm_notes: Both claims revolve around the same central concern about an undefined parameter critical to experiments.
llm_rationale: Both claims identify a missing or undefined r parameter in the experimental setup, which undermines evaluation and clarity. The first reviewer notes that section 3.2 mentions an r parameter that is not defined, hindering evaluation in section 4.1; the second reviewer explicitly states that the r parameter is important for experiments but not identified in section 3.2. These concern the same core issue (missing/undefined r parameter affecting experimental validity) and are aligned in focus and severity.
matched_claim_text: There is a concern that the r parameter is important for experiments but not identified in section 3.2
source_sentence:
The biggest weakness in the paper seems to be that a critical part of it is missing. The experimental setup in section 4.1 states 
> We control the size of the local neighborhood by controlling the diagonal lengths via the $r$ parameter mentioned in section 3.2

But section 3.2 makes no mention of a parameter $r$, and it is difficult to make a full evaluation of the experiments in section 4 absent a definition of $r$.
title: Deep Network Partition Density Exhibits Double Descent

claim_text: Equation 4 describes the neural network construction. However, I am unclear about the objective function to optimize the neural network.
llm_notes: Partial consensus label selected due to overlap in topic but not identical concern.
llm_rationale: Both claims concern the objective/likelihood function and how the neural network and its approximation are defined (Eq. 4 and VI in Ou et al. 2022). However, one claim questions the objective function to optimize, while the other asks how the variational approximation q(Y|S,V) relates to p(S|V). They pertain to different aspects: one about the objective function vs. the probabilistic modeling/approximation. They are about related but distinct concerns in the same methodological area.
matched_claim_text: It looks like learning the neural network approximation in Eq. (4) is done via variational inference as in Ou et al. (2022). As I am not familiar with the cited work, it is unclear to me how $q(Y|S,V)$ is serves as an approximation for the subset likelihood $p(S|V)$ when the former is a distribution over $Y$ and the latter is a distribution over $S$.
source_sentence: 1. Equation 4 describes the neural network construction. However, I am unclear about the objective function to optimize the neural network. Also, after optimization, how do you use this neural network to select a subset?
title: Enhancing Neural Subset Selection: Integrating Background Information into Set Representations
